message.content: Adversaries may modify the system image of embedded network devices to weaken defenses and gain new capabilities. These devices often have monolithic operating systems, where most functionalities are contained within a single file. By altering this file, attackers can either make immediate changes by modifying it in memory during runtime or implement changes on the next boot by modifying it in storage. The potential impact includes compromised device integrity, unauthorized access, and persistent control over the device. Common exploitation methods involve replacing or altering the system image file to introduce malicious code or backdoors. Mitigation strategies include implementing secure boot mechanisms, regularly verifying the integrity of system images, and employing robust access controls to prevent unauthorized modifications.
